outgoing_bearing_from_turn_value_query: Find the outgoing bearing marked ?. Use the incoming bearing, the shown left/right turn angle, and the north reference.
Determine the missing outgoing bearing after the shown station turn. Bearings are measured clockwise from north.
Use the traverse turn note and the north reference to compute the bearing marked ?.
Find the bearing of the outgoing line after applying the visible left/right turn at the middle station.
Use the incoming survey bearing and the marked turn angle to determine the missing outgoing bearing.
station_elevation_value_query: Find the station elevation marked ?. Use the benchmark elevation, backsight, and foresight readings in the field note.
Compute the missing station elevation from the leveling field note.
Use the benchmark elevation and staff readings to determine the elevation marked ?.
Find the target station elevation by using the benchmark, backsight, and foresight values shown.
Use the leveling sketch and field note to compute the missing elevation marked ?.
traverse_area_value_query: Find the area marked ?. Use the chainage intervals along the baseline and the perpendicular offsets in the field note.
Compute the area between the baseline and offset line by summing the trapezoids between adjacent stations.
Use each adjacent pair of chainages and offsets to find the total offset-traverse area marked ?.
Find the marked survey area from the baseline chainages and perpendicular offsets.
Use the offset sketch and field note to compute the total area between the baseline and the shown offset line.

single: Use the bearing labels as degrees clockwise from north.
Apply the shown turn to the incoming bearing.
Use the left/right turn direction exactly as shown.
Normalize the final bearing to a value from 0 to 359 degrees.
Use the visible turn arc and bearing note.
leveling_station_elevation: First add backsight to the benchmark elevation, then subtract foresight for the target station.
Use height of instrument from the benchmark and backsight, then apply the foresight reading.
Compute benchmark elevation plus backsight minus foresight.
Use the same elevation units shown in the field note.
The answer is the target station elevation, not the height of instrument.
offset_trapezoid_area: For each adjacent station pair, multiply the chainage interval by the average of the two offsets, then add the results.
Each neighboring pair of offsets forms one trapezoid over the baseline interval.
Use only adjacent chainage intervals; do not multiply total baseline length by one average offset.
Add the area contributions from all adjacent station intervals.
The answer is the total area between the baseline and the offset line.

scene:survey_traverse_scene.object_description: a survey field sketch with a diagram and a field-note panel

global.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with exactly two keys: "annotation" and "answer".
global.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with exactly one key: "answer".

task:outgoing_bearing_from_turn_value_qu.answer_hint: set "answer" to the marked survey bearing as an integer number of degrees clockwise from north

task:station_elevation_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the missing station elevation as an integer value in the same units shown

task:traverse_area_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the marked survey area as an integer number of square units
